qtype: Ratio, percentage of amount, percentage decrease, reverse percentage, speed/distance
tex:
(a) In a cycling club, the number of members are in the ratio males : females = 8 : 3.
\newline
 The club has 342 females.
\newline

(i) (b) Find the total number of members.
\newline

(c) Find the percentage of the total number of members that are female.
\newline

(d) The price of a bicycle is \$1020.
\newline
 Club members receive a 15\% discount on this price.
\newline

Find how much a club member pays for this bicycle.
\newline

(e) In 2019, the membership fee of the cycling club is \$79.50 .
\newline
 This is 6\% more than last year.
\newline

Find the increase in the cost of the membership.
\newline

(f) Asif cycles a distance of 105 km.
\newline
 On the first part of his journey he cycles 60 km in 2 hours 24 minutes.
\newline
 On the second part of his journey he cycles 45 km at 20 km/h.
\newline

Find his average speed for the whole journey.
\newline

(g) Bryan invested \$480 in an account 4 years ago.
\newline
 The account pays compound interest at a rate of 2.1\% per year.
\newline
 Today, he uses some of the money in this account to buy a bicycle costing \$430.
\newline

Calculate how much money remains in his account.
\newline

(h) The formula $s = \frac{1}{2}at^2$ is used to calculate the distance, $s$, travelled by a bicycle.
\newline
 When $a = 3$ and $t = 10$, each correct to the nearest integer, calculate the lower bound of the distance, $s$.
\newline

In a cycling club, the number of members are in the ratio males : females = 8 : 3.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}The club has 342 females.
\newline

Find the total number of members.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

Find how much a club member pays for this bicycle.
\newline

\newline

\newline

Find the increase in the cost of the membership.
\newline

Asif cycles a distance of 105 km.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}On the first part of his journey he cycles 60 km in 2 hours 24 minutes.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}On the second part of his journey he cycles 45 km at 20 km/h.
\newline

Find his average speed for the whole journey.
\newline

Bryan invested \$480 in an account 4 years ago.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}The account pays compound interest at a rate of 2.1\% per year.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}Today, he uses some of the money in this account to buy a bicycle costing \$430.
\newline

Calculate how much money remains in his account.
\newline

The formula $s = \frac{1}{2}at^2$ is used to calculate the distance, $s$, travelled by a bicycle.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}When $a = 3$ and $t = 10$, each correct to the nearest integer, calculate the lower bound of the distance, $s$.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item In a cycling club, the number of members are in the ratio males : females = 8 : 3.
\noindent \hspace{1em}The club has 342 females.

\begin{subparts}
\item Find the total number of members.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item Find the percentage of the total number of members that are female.

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{1}

\end{subparts}

\item The price of a bicycle is \$1020.
\noindent \hspace{1em}Club members receive a 15\% discount on this price.

Find how much a club member pays for this bicycle.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\item In 2019, the membership fee of the cycling club is \$79.50 .
\noindent \hspace{1em}This is 6\% more than last year.

Find the increase in the cost of the membership.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item Asif cycles a distance of 105 km.
\noindent \hspace{1em}On the first part of his journey he cycles 60 km in 2 hours 24 minutes.
\noindent \hspace{1em}On the second part of his journey he cycles 45 km at 20 km/h.

Find his average speed for the whole journey.

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{4}

\item Bryan invested \$480 in an account 4 years ago.
\noindent \hspace{1em}The account pays compound interest at a rate of 2.1\% per year.
\noindent \hspace{1em}Today, he uses some of the money in this account to buy a bicycle costing \$430.

Calculate how much money remains in his account.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item The formula $s = \frac{1}{2}at^2$ is used to calculate the distance, $s$, travelled by a bicycle.
\noindent \hspace{1em}When $a = 3$ and $t = 10$, each correct to the nearest integer, calculate the lower bound of the distance, $s$.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\end{parts}

qtype: simultaneous linear equations and algebraic manipulation
tex:
(a) The diagram shows a triangle and a quadrilateral.
\newline
 All angles are in degrees.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(i) (b) For the triangle, show that $3a + 5b = 170$.
\newline

(c) For the quadrilateral, show that $9a + 7b = 310$.
\newline

(d) Solve these simultaneous equations.
\newline
 Show all your working.
\newline

(e) Find the size of the smallest angle in the triangle.
\newline

(f) Solve the equation $6x - 3 = -12$.
\newline

(g) Rearrange $2(4x - y) = 5x - 3$ to make $y$ the subject.
\newline

(h) Simplify.
\newline
$$ (27x^9)^{\frac{2}{3}} $$

(i) Simplify.
\newline
$$ \frac{x^2 + 5x}{x^2 - 25} $$

content.content:
For the triangle, show that $3a + 5b = 170$.
\newline

For the quadrilateral, show that $9a + 7b = 310$.
\newline

Solve these simultaneous equations.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}Show all your working.
\newline

Find the size of the smallest angle in the triangle.
\newline

Solve the equation $6x - 3 = -12$.
\newline

Rearrange $2(4x - y) = 5x - 3$ to make $y$ the subject.
\newline

Simplify.
\newline
$$ (27x^9)^{\frac{2}{3}} $$

Simplify.
\newline
$$ \frac{x^2 + 5x}{x^2 - 25} $$

qtype: graph of a cubic function and its applications
tex:
The table shows some values for $y = x^3 + x^2 - 5x$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c||c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-3$ & $-2$ & $-1.5$ & $-1$ & $0$ & $1$ & $1.5$ & $2$ & $2.5$ & $3$ \\
\hline
$y$ & $-3$ & $6$ & $6.4$ & & $0$ & & $-1.9$ & $2$ & $9.4$ & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Complete the table.
\newline

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^3 + x^2 - 5x$ for $-3 \leqslant x \leqslant 3$.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(c) Use your graph to solve the equation $x^3 + x^2 - 5x = 0$.
\newline

(d) By drawing a suitable tangent, find an estimate of the gradient of the curve at $x = 2$.
\newline

(e) Write down the largest value of the integer, $k$, so that the equation $x^3 + x^2 - 5x = k$ has three solutions for $-3 \leqslant x \leqslant 3$.
\newline

Complete the table.
\newline

\newline

Write down the largest value of the integer, $k$, so that the equation $x^3 + x^2 - 5x = k$ has three solutions for $-3 \leqslant x \leqslant 3$.
\newline

tex:
The diagram shows a field, $ABCD$, on horizontal ground.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) There is a vertical post at $C$.
\newline
 From $B$, the angle of elevation of the top of the post is $19^\circ$.
\newline

Find the height of the post.
\newline

(b) Use the cosine rule to find angle $BAC$.
\newline

(c) Use the sine rule to find angle $CAD$.
\newline

(d) Calculate the area of the field.
\newline

(e) The bearing of $D$ from $A$ is $070^\circ$.
\newline

Find the bearing of $A$ from $C$.
\newline

content.content:
There is a vertical post at $C$.
\newline

\newline

Find the height of the post.
\newline

Use the cosine rule to find angle $BAC$.
\newline

Use the sine rule to find angle $CAD$.
\newline

Calculate the area of the field.
\newline

The bearing of $D$ from $A$ is $070^\circ$.
\newline

Find the bearing of $A$ from $C$.
\newline

tex:
The cumulative frequency diagram shows information about the distance, $d$ km, travelled by each of 60 male cyclists in one weekend.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Use the cumulative frequency diagram to find an estimate of

(i) (b) the median,

(c) the lower quartile,

(d) the interquartile range.
\newline

(e) For the same weekend, the interquartile range for the distances travelled by a group of female cyclists is 40 km.
\newline

Make one comment comparing the distribution of the distances travelled by the males with the distribution of the distances travelled by the females.
\newline

(f) A male cyclist is chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that he travelled more than 50 km.
\newline

(g) (i) (h) Use the cumulative frequency diagram to complete this frequency table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Distance ($d$ km) & Number of male cyclists \\
\hline
$0 < d \leqslant 40$ & $18$ \\
\hline
$40 < d \leqslant 50$ & $9$ \\
\hline
$50 < d \leqslant 60$ & \\
\hline
$60 < d \leqslant 70$ & \\
\hline
$70 < d \leqslant 90$ & \\
\hline
$90 < d \leqslant 120$ & $2$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) Calculate an estimate of the mean distance travelled.
\newline

tex:
(a) A bag contains 4 red marbles and 2 yellow marbles.
\newline
 Behnaz picks two marbles at random without replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that

(i) (b) the marbles are both red,

(c) the marbles are not both red.
\newline

(d) Another bag contains 5 blue marbles and 2 green marbles.
\newline
 Bryn picks one marble at random without replacement.
\newline
 If this marble is not green, he picks another marble at random without replacement.
\newline
 He continues until he picks a green marble.
\newline

Find the probability that he picks a green marble on his first, second or third attempt.
\newline

content.content:
A bag contains 4 red marbles and 2 yellow marbles.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}Behnaz picks two marbles at random without replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that
the marbles are both red,

the marbles are not both red.
\newline

Another bag contains 5 blue marbles and 2 green marbles.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}Bryn picks one marble at random without replacement.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}If this marble is not green, he picks another marble at random without replacement.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}He continues until he picks a green marble.
\newline

Find the probability that he picks a green marble on his first, second or third attempt.
\newline

qtype: functions (composite, inverse) and solving related equations
tex:
$$ f(x) = 2x - 3  g(x) = 9 - x^2  h(x) = 3^x $$

(a) Find

(i) (b) $f(4)$,

(c) $hg(3)$,

(d) $g(2x)$ in its simplest form,

(e) $fg(x)$ in its simplest form.
\newline

(f) Find $f^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

(g) Find $x$ when $5f(x) = 3$.
\newline

(h) Solve the equation $gf(x) = -16$.
\newline

(i) Find $x$ when $h^{-1}(x) = -2$.
\newline

content.content: Find
$g(2x)$ in its simplest form,

$fg(x)$ in its simplest form.
\newline

Find $f^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $5f(x) = 3$.
\newline

Solve the equation $gf(x) = -16$.
\newline

Find $x$ when $h^{-1}(x) = -2$.
\newline

tex:
Solve.
\newline
$$ \frac{1}{x} - \frac{2}{x+1} = 3 $$
Show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

stem.content:
Solve.
\newline
$$ \frac{1}{x} - \frac{2}{x+1} = 3 $$
Show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

_rawBody:
Solve.
$$ \frac{1}{x} - \frac{2}{x+1} = 3 $$
Show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.

\AnswerLine[$x=$][ or $x=$]
\Marks{7}

tex:
%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

$OAB$ is a triangle and $ABC$ and $PQC$ are straight lines.
\newline
 $P$ is the midpoint of $OA$, $Q$ is the midpoint of $PC$ and $OQ : QB = 3 : 1$.
\newline
 $\overrightarrow{OA} = 4\mathbf{a}$ and $\overrightarrow{OB} = 8\mathbf{b}$.
\newline

(a) Find, in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and/or $\mathbf{b}$, in its simplest form

(i) (b) $\overrightarrow{AB}$,

(c) $\overrightarrow{OQ}$,

(d) $\overrightarrow{PQ}$.
\newline

(e) By using vectors, find the ratio $AB : BC$.
\newline

content.content: Find, in terms of $\mathbf{a}$ and/or $\mathbf{b}$, in its simplest form
$\overrightarrow{AB}$,
$\overrightarrow{OQ}$,

$\overrightarrow{PQ}$.
\newline

By using vectors, find the ratio $AB : BC$.
\newline
